text:
The 2024 Paris Olympic games are officially underway as athletes from around the world compete in France.

Thirteen athletes who attendedMichigan high schoolswill compete for Olympic glory in the 2024 Paris summer games. For many of these athletes, competing in Michiganhigh school sportswas a part of their journey to the 2024 Paris Olympics.

Here are the athletes fromMichigancompeting in the 2024 Paris Olympics.

Abby Tamer ГўВЂВ“ Whitmore Lake ГўВЂВ“ Field Hockey

Tamer is a graduate fromDexter High School, and he was a four-time Michigan All-State first team selection (2017, '18, '19, '20). Tamer is a senior at the University of Michigan, and she's been on the NFHCA Collegiate National Academic Team.Г‚

Team USA womenГўВЂВ™s field hockey begins on July 27 against Argentina at 1:45 p.m. ET.

Devin Booker ГўВЂВ“ Grand Rapids ГўВЂВ“ Basketball

Booker was born in Grand Rapids, and he attendedGrandville High Schooluntil he moved to Mississippi before his sophomore season. Booker played on freshman, junior varsity and varsity for Grandville during his freshman year.

Heath Baldwin ГўВЂВ“ Kalamazoo ГўВЂВ“ Track and Field

Baldwin is a Kalamazoo native who went toHackett Catholic Central. Baldwin won the decathlon at the 2024 U.S. Olympic Trials in Eugene, Oregon. He started his career at the University of Michigan before he transferred to Michigan State.

ГўВЂВњIГўВЂВ™m pretty young. And if I can get a medal and gain some experience, too, I think that'll be a win. But I've gone into every meet this year hoping to win. So I mean, obviously, the main goal is to take my best shot at winning,ГўВЂВќ Baldwin told theLansing State Journal.Г‚

The decathlon begins on August 2 at 4:05 a.m.

Adam Coon ГўВЂВ“ Fowlerville ГўВЂВ“ Wrestling

Coon will compete in the 130kg weight class in the Paris Olympics. He attendedFowlerville High School, where he was a four-time state champion, winning at 215 pounds (2010, '11) and 285 pounds (2012, '13).

Greco-Roman 130kg wrestling begins on August 5 at 9 a.m.

Aaron Cummings ГўВЂВ“ Muskegon ГўВЂВ“ Rugby

Cummings started his rugby career atGrand Haven High School. He excelled at Davenport University, and he plays professionally for the Chicago Lions.Г‚

Rugby 7s began on July 24.

Andrew Evans ГўВЂВ“ Portage ГўВЂВ“ Track and Field

Evans is aPortage Northern High Schoolgraduate competing in the discus throw. He was first at the U.S. Olympic Trials with a throw of 218 feet and six inches. It will be his second Olympics. He qualified in 2016 for the Rio Olympics.Г‚

MenГўВЂВ™s discus throw begins on August 5 at 4:10 p.m.

Grant Fisher ГўВЂВ“ Grand Blanc ГўВЂВ“ Track and Field

TheGrand Blancnative ran a sub-4 mile while in high school, and he was a two-time Foot Locker national cross country champion. Fisher competed in the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, and he finished fifth in the 10000m and ninth in the 5000m. HeГўВЂВ™ll be running the same events in 2024.

The menГўВЂВ™s 10000m will be on August 2 at 3:20 p.m. The menГўВЂВ™s 5000m begins on August 7 at 5:10 a.m.

Zach Hammer ГўВЂВ“ Ann Arbor ГўВЂВ“ Sport Climbing

Hammer graduated fromSkyline High Schoolthis summer. He will compete in speed climbing at the Paris Olympics. ItГўВЂВ™s the second time speed climbing will be an Olympic sport having debuted at the Tokyo Olympics.

Speed climbing begins on August 6 at 7 a.m.

Hobbs Kessler ГўВЂВ“ Ann Arbor ГўВЂВ“ Track and Field

Kessler is the second Skyline High School graduate in the 2024 Paris Olympics. He broke the U.S. high school indoor mile record while he was an Eagle. Kessler trains in Ann Arbor for the Very Nice Track Club.Г‚

Kessler will compete in the 1500m. The first round is on August 2 at 5:05 a.m.

Sorin Koszyk ГўВЂВ“ Grosse Pointe Park ГўВЂВ“ Rowing

Koszyk started his rowing career as a freshman while atGrosse Pointe South High School. He rowed for four years at the Detroit Boat Club before rowing for Cornell, where he was First Team All Ivy League.Г‚

Koszyk will compete in the menГўВЂВ™s double sculls which begins on July 27 at 5:30 a.m.

Alena Olsen ГўВЂВ“ Caledonia ГўВЂВ“ Rugby

Olsen didnГўВЂВ™t begin playing rugby until she attended the University of Michigan. She became an All-American during her time as a Wolverine.Г‚

WomenГўВЂВ™s rugby begins on July 28.

Hannah Robersts ГўВЂВ“ Buchanan ГўВЂВ“ Cycling

Roberts attendedBuchanan High School, and sheГўВЂВ™ll represent Team USA in womenГўВЂВ™s park BMX freestyle. Roberts won a silver medal in the event in the 2020 Tokyo Olympics.

WomenГўВЂВ™s park BMX freestyle begins on July 20 at 7:25 a.m.

Grace Stark ГўВЂВ“ White Bear ГўВЂВ“ Track and Field

Stark attendedLakeland High Schooland won three individual state championships. She attended the University of Florida, and she finished her career as a 2024 NCAA outdoor champion in the 100m hurdles.

The womenГўВЂВ™s 100m hurdles begins on August 2 at 4:35 a.m.

title: Michigan high school athletes competing in 2024 Paris Summer Olympics
description: 13 former Michigan high school athletes are competing in the 2024 Olympics
